ЪҶШ¬ЩҲШұЫҢ ШӘШөШ§ЩҲЫҢШұ ШіШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШұЩҲ Ш§ШІ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ШӘШҙШ®ЫҢШө ШЁШҜЫҢЩ…Шҹрҹ§җ

рҹҶ” @akhbarenfejari
posts.keywords: Detecting AI-generated images, reverse image search
posts.audio_en:
These days we've been witnessing the spread of videos and images вҖ” we were in some virtual spaces dealing with protests where the content being played was fake. Original tweets were saying certain things were fake, and they became clearly self-documented by the day. You can find specific things вҖ” for example, there's a lot going on inside it, but it hasn't been well-ridden [i.e., well-examined], it's there with its own finger [i.e., fingerprints/traces]. Zoom in, zoom in on these fingerprints. Usually where the edges are, look carefully, do it in writing. Look вҖ” whether it's outside or without a body or lifeless вҖ” or [zoom in] a hundred times, again and again.
Use a reverse image search. When you upload something to one place, it gives you a source from another place. We can вҖ” if the video and the news [are checked] вҖ” [run it through] tools like Google Lens or Metadata or in the comments on social media, platforms like X [Twitter] that are doing this work. Look at the dark shadow вҖ” what direction it gives вҖ” if the work has been done, if it's been coded, say that it's there, it's trapped [i.e., it's been manipulated].
Also in the credits and timestamps, again the same trap. That вҖ” yes вҖ” it's possible, I respectfully ask, look carefully at this side, at this edge, at what's being given вҖ” the century/era that has passed. Sometimes from your own era they load it back again if there's something to be seen. They go from the original source, they do the work from the original source вҖ” again the same placement. Every other [tool] in Persian does fifteen [checks] вҖ” sometimes it does, sometimes the text [reveals it]
